title: Oil Prices Go Up and Stocks Go Down in Asia
summary: Oil prices went up a lot and stock markets in Asia went down on Monday. This happened because the war between the US, Israel, and Iran is now in its fifth week. The price of Brent crude oil rose above $115 a barrel. In Japan and South Korea, stock markets fell by almost 3%. Iran-backed groups attacked Israel over the weekend, and Iran threatened more strikes. US President Donald Trump said he might take oil from Iran. A shipping expert warned that the war could make oil and food prices go up even more, affecting poor countries. This conflict is causing big changes in global markets.

keywords.explanation: the cost of oil, which went up because of the war
places where people buy and sell shares of companies, which went down in Asia
a fight between countries, here involving the US, Israel, and Iran
a country in the Middle East that is part of the conflict
a person who knows a lot about moving goods by sea

options: Oil and food prices going up
questions.correct_answer: Oil and food prices going up

questions.question: How long has the war been going on?

background_read: This article talks about how a war between the US, Israel, and Iran is affecting the world. Oil is a type of fuel used for cars and heating, and when there's a war in places where oil comes from, prices can go up. Stock markets show how well companies are doing, and when people worry about wars, stocks often go down. The war started because of attacks and threats, and leaders like the US president are talking about taking oil. Experts warn that this could make things more expensive, especially for poor countries.
Article_Structure: WHO: US, Israel, Iran, Iran-backed groups, US President Donald Trump, shipping expert Lars Jensen
WHAT: Oil prices rose and stock markets fell in Asia due to the war entering its fifth week, with threats and attacks escalating
WHERE: Asia (Japan, South Korea), Middle East (Iran, Strait of Hormuz), global markets
WHY: Because the conflict is causing uncertainty, leading to higher oil prices and lower stocks, with warnings about further economic impacts

perspectives.description: The war is making oil and food prices go up, hurting global markets and poor countries.
